tasks.name: Choose a base IPTV plan with 100+ channels under $20/month billed yearly
tasks.description: Select a subscription plan that costs less than $20 per month, includes at least 100 channels, is billed annually, and proceed to the checkout page with that plan selected.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the "Plans" option in the top navigation menu.
In the price filter section, enter "20" into the maximum monthly price field.
In the channel count filter, select the option labeled "100+ channels".
In the billing cycle toggle, select the "Yearly" or "Annual" option.
Click on the sort dropdown and choose "Price: Low to High".
Click the "Select Plan" or "Choose Plan" button on the first plan in the sorted list that meets the filters.
On the plan summary page, click the "Proceed to Checkout" button.

tasks.name: Add a sports add-on under $10/month with 5+ HD sports channels to an existing plan
tasks.description: Find and add a sports add-on that costs less than $10 per month and includes at least 5 HD sports channels, then save the updated plan configuration.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Add-ons" link in the main navigation.
On the add-ons page, click the "Sports" category tab or tile.
In the monthly price filter, set the maximum price field to "10".
In the features or quality filter, check the checkbox labeled "HD channels".
Scroll through the sports add-ons and click on the first add-on whose description indicates "5+ HD sports channels" or similar wording.
On the selected add-on details page, click the "Add to My Plan" or "Include in Subscription" button.
In the panel or page showing your updated plan, click the "View Plan Summary" or "Review Changes" button.
On the plan summary page, click the "Save Changes" or "Confirm" button to finalize adding the sports add-on.

tasks.name: Favorite 3 channels: 1 English movie, 1 Spanish news, and 1 all-ages kids channel
tasks.description: Locate and add three specific types of channels to the favorites list: one English-language movie channel, one Spanish-language news channel, and one kids channel rated for all ages.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Channels" or "Guide" option in the main navigation.
In the channel filters, set the genre to "Movies" and the language to "English", then click on the first movie channel in the results list.
On this movie channel’s page, click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon to favorite the channel.
Use the "Back to Channels" link or navigation to return to the main channels list.
Change the filters so that the genre is set to "News" and the language is set to "Spanish", then click on the second channel in the results list.
On this Spanish news channel’s page, click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon to favorite the channel.
Return again to the main channels list and set the genre filter to "Kids" and the rating filter to "All Ages" or "TV-G", then click on the first kids channel in the results list.
On this kids channel’s page, click the "Add to Favorites" or heart icon, then click the "Favorites" or "My Channels" link in the header or account menu to open the favorites list.

tasks.name: Set up parental controls with a PIN and TV-14 limit and block purchases
tasks.description: Sign in, create a 4-digit parental PIN set to 1234, restrict content to TV-14 and below, and enable a setting that blocks purchases without entering the PIN.
steps: On the homepage, click the "Sign In" button in the top-right corner.
In the email field of the sign-in form, type "parent@example.com".
In the password field, type "Parent123!".
Click the "Sign In" or "Log In" button on the form.
After signing in, click the profile icon in the header and select "Settings" from the dropdown menu.
In the settings sidebar, click on the "Parental Controls" option.
In the "Create PIN" field, enter "1234" and in the "Confirm PIN" field, enter "1234" again.
In the rating limit dropdown or slider, select "TV-14" as the maximum allowed rating.
Toggle on the switch or checkbox labeled "Block purchases without PIN" or similar wording.

tasks.name: Upgrade to the plan with more simultaneous streams under $30/month
tasks.description: Compare at least two eligible plans under $30 per month and switch to the one that offers the higher number of simultaneous streams.
steps: From the signed-in dashboard, click the "Change Plan" or "Manage Plan" link near your current plan information.
On the plans page, use the price filter to show only plans with a monthly cost of "30" or less.
In the filtered plans list, click the "Compare" checkbox for the first two plans displayed.
Click the "Compare Selected" button to open the comparison view.
In the comparison table, click the "Select" or "Choose" button under the plan that shows a larger number in the "Simultaneous Streams" or "Streams at once" row.
On the plan change confirmation page, select the "Start immediately" or equivalent radio button for when to apply the change.
Click the "Confirm Upgrade" or "Update Plan" button to finalize the upgrade.
After the confirmation screen appears, click the "Go to My Account" or "Return to Dashboard" button.

tasks.name: Schedule a recording of a sports program next Friday at 8:00 PM and keep it for 7 days
tasks.description: Use the TV guide to find any sports program that starts at 8:00 PM on the next Friday and schedule a recording that is set to be kept for 7 days.
steps: From the dashboard or homepage, click the "TV Guide" or "Live TV" link in the main navigation.
In the guide’s date selector, click on the tab or button corresponding to next Friday’s date.
In the guide filters, select the genre filter labeled "Sports".
Scroll through the grid to find a sports channel row that has a program starting at "8:00 PM" on that Friday.
Click on the program block in the guide that corresponds to the 8:00 PM sports program.
In the program details pop-up or side panel, click the "Record" or "Schedule Recording" button.
In the recording options, open the "Keep until" dropdown and select the option labeled "7 days" or "Keep for 7 days".
Click the "Save Recording" or "Confirm" button to finalize the scheduled recording.

tasks.name: Switch to annual billing, apply promo code SAVE20, and select a plan under $200/year
tasks.description: Change the billing cycle to annual, apply the promo code SAVE20, then choose an annual plan whose displayed total annual cost is less than $200 and update the subscription.
steps: From the signed-in dashboard, click on the profile icon and select "Billing" or "Billing & Payments" from the menu.
On the billing page, locate the "Billing Cycle" section and select the "Annual" radio button or toggle option.
In the promo code or coupon field, type "SAVE20".
Click the "Apply" button next to the promo code field.
In the list of available annual plans, use any sort control to choose "Annual total: Low to High" or equivalent sorting.
From the sorted plans, click the "Select" or "Choose Plan" button on the first plan where the displayed annual total is less than "$200".
Scroll down and click the "Update Subscription" or "Save Changes" button to confirm the new billing cycle and plan.
After the update completes, click the "Return to Billing Overview" or similar link to go back to the billing summary page.

tasks.name: Find a 4K nature documentary series and start playing the first episode
tasks.description: Search for a nature-related series, filter results to show only 4K documentary series, and start playback of the first episode of the top matching series.
steps: From the homepage, click on the search bar in the header area.
Type "nature" into the search field and press Enter or click the search icon.
In the search results filters, select "4K" under the "Resolution" or "Quality" section.
In the content type filter, choose the "Series" option.
Click on the sort dropdown and select "Most Popular" or "Top Results".
From the sorted results, click on the first title whose genre or tag includes the word "Documentary".
On the series detail page, scroll down to the episodes list and locate Episode 1 in the first season.
Click the "Play" button on the row for Episode 1 to start playback.
